zIInternal utility to determine if the frame is special or
        limited.TF)
find_sal

DUMMY_FRAME
SIGTRAMP_FRAME)
frame
salr
_is_limited_frame@
z FrameDecorator._is_limited_framec
zLReturn any elided frames that this class might be
        wrapping, or None.
elidedN)
hasattrr
FrameDecorator.elidedc
Return the name of the frame's function or an address of
        the function of the frame.  First determine if this is a
        special frame.  If not, try to determine filename from GDB's
        frame internal function API.  Finally, if a name cannot be
        determined return the address.  If this function returns an
        address, GDB will attempt to determine the function name from
        its internal minimal symbols store (for example, for inferiors
        without debug-info).
functionz
<function called from gdb>z
<signal handler called>N)
isinstancer
Framer
inferior_framer
str)
funcr
FrameDecorator.functionc
z$Return the address of the frame's pc
address)
FrameDecorator.addressc
Return the filename associated with this frame, detecting
        and returning the appropriate library name is this is a shared
        library.r
N)	r
solib_name)
FrameDecorator.filenamec
Return an iterable of frame arguments for this frame, if
        any.  The iterable object contains objects conforming with the
        Symbol/Value interface.  If there are no frame arguments, or
        if this frame is deemed to be a special case, return None.
frame_argsN)
	FrameVars
fetch_frame_args
argsr
FrameDecorator.frame_argsc
Return an iterable of local variables for this frame, if
        any.  The iterable object contains objects conforming with the
        Symbol/Value interface.  If there are no frame locals, or if
        this frame is deemed to be a special case, return None.
frame_localsN)
fetch_frame_localsr"
FrameDecorator.frame_localsc
Return line number information associated with the frame's
        pc.  If symbol table/line information does not exist, or if
        this frame is deemed to be a special case, return None
lineN)
FrameDecorator.linec

FrameVars.__init__c
Local utility method to determine if according to Symbol
        type whether it should be included in the iterator.  Not all
        symbols are fetched, and only symbols that return
        True from this method should be fetched.TF)
basestringZ

FrameVars.fetch_bc
r@qt|
rPqD|
Public utility method to fetch frame local variables for
        the stored frame.  Frame arguments are not fetched.  If there
        are no frame local variables, return an empty list.N)

FrameVars.fetch_frame_localsc
r>qF|
s^qR|
Public utility method to fetch frame arguments for the
        stored frame.  Frame arguments are the only type fetched.  If
        there are no frame argument variables, return an empty list.N)
